case_title: PROFESSIONAL PHARMACY ALLIANCE A CORPORATION VS. TOBY DOUGLAS

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS FOR THEIR PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE BECAUSE PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT RESPONDENTS FAILED TO PERFORM A MINISTERIAL DUTY. (SEE VENICE TOWN COUNCIL, INC. V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1996) 47 CAL.APP.4TH 113, 138.) WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE 10000 DOES NOT CREATE A MANDATORY MINISTERIAL DUTY FOR WHICH RESPONDENTS ARE REQUIRED TO PERFORM. EVEN IF PETITIONERS COULD SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS, THEY CANNOT ESTABLISH IRREPARABLE INJURY. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT RESPONDENTS HAVE EXEMPTED A LARGE QUANTITY OF DRUG PRODUCTS FROM THE 10% PROVIDER PAYMENT REDUCTIONS, WHICH WILL REDUCE THE FINANCIAL IMPACT TO PETITIONERS. (SEE DECLARATION OF HENDRIX IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION, 12-17.) FURTHER, PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEY CANNOT BE FULLY COMPENSATED BY THE PAYMENT OF DAMAGES FOR THEIR ALLEGED INJURY. BALANCING THE EQUITIES, THERE ARE PUBLIC POLICY CONSIDERATIONS AGAINST ENJOINING PUBLIC AGENCIES FROM PERFORMING THEIR DUTIES. (SEE TAHOE KEYS PROPERTY OWNERS' ASSN. V. STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BD. (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 1459, 1471.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Off Calendar

Petitioner'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-31-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NATALLIA MAZINA OF MEDICAID DEFENSE FUND, TEL. NO. 457-3984 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. GREGORY D. BROWN, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5461 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner's Motion For Preliminary Injunction IS Denied. Petitioners have not shown a likelihood of success on the merits for their petition for writ of mandate because Petitioners have not shown that Respondents failed to perform a ministerial duty. (See Venice Town Council, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 113, 138.) Welfare & Institutions Code 10000 does not create a mandatory ministerial duty for which Respondents are required to perform. Even if Petitioners could show a likelihood of success, they cannot establish irreparable injury. The evidence shows that Respondents have exempted a large quantity of drug products from the 10% provider payment reductions, which will reduce the financial impact to Petitioners. (See Declaration of Hendrix in Support of Opposition, 12-17.) Further, Petitioners have not shown that they cannot be fully compensated by the payment of damages for their alleged injury. Balancing the equities, there are public policy considerations against enjoining public agencies from performing their duties. (See Tahoe Keys Property Owners' Assn. v. State Water Resources Control Bd. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1471.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EG
